root_cause.text: The dimension update logic incorrectly required both width and height to change simultaneously, failing to update dimensions if only one changed and leading to inadequate buffer allocation.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces `&&` with `||` in the condition `if (s->width != avctx->width && s->height != avctx->height)`, ensuring `ff_set_dimensions` is called when either the width or the height changes.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The dimension update logic incorrectly required both width and height to change simultaneously, failing to update dimensions if only one changed and leading to inadequate buffer allocation.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces `&&` with `||` in the condition `if (s->width != avctx->width && s->height != avctx->height)`, ensuring `ff_set_dimensions` is called when either the width or the height changes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix dimension update logic to trigger when either width or height changes.
intent_record.what: Changed the logical AND operator (&&) to a logical OR operator (||) in the dimension check.
intent_record.how: Ensures that the codec dimensions are updated if either the width or the height changes, rather than incorrectly requiring both to change simultaneously.

grouping_result.intent: Fix dimension update logic to trigger when either width or height changes.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly fixes the root cause by modifying the dimension update logic to trigger when either width or height changes, rather than requiring both to change simultaneously.

candidates.rationale: This conditional incorrectly requires both width and height to change simultaneously for the dimensions to be updated, which directly matches the root cause.

vuln_statements.explanation: This condition incorrectly requires both width and height to change simultaneously to update dimensions, which perfectly matches the described root cause.

bic_trace_step.reason: The function `decode_frame` contains the vulnerable logic `if (s->width != avctx->width && s->height != avctx->height) {`. This requires both width and height to change simultaneously to update the dimensions, which matches the vulnerability root cause. The fix (using `||` instead of `&&`) is not present.
